symptom: Photo shows cloth-insulated single conductors on ceramic knobs or tubes, often mixed with newer cable or an open splice.
diagnosis: Legacy knob-and-tube wiring with possible uncontained splice or mixed wiring method. Main concern is brittle insulation, unsupported alterations, and lack of grounding conductor.
procedure: Stop before moving the conductors; old insulation can crack when flexed.
Identify the source circuit and turn it off at the breaker or fuse.
Verify dead with a meter on each conductor to ground and conductor to conductor.
Look for open splices, buried splices, insulation contact, and mixed cable transitions.
Recommend proper junction-box containment or circuit replacement where needed.
safety_notes: Do not handle energized cloth-insulated conductors.
Do not bury knob-and-tube wiring in insulation or finished cavities.
If insulation crumbles or copper is exposed, stop and repair before proceeding.
